name: Elizabeth (N.J.) Firemen'S
description_short: Federal credit union in Elizabeth, NJ serving firefighters and government employees with $7.3M in assets, checking/savings accounts, and payday alternative loans.
description_long:
Elizabeth (N.J.) Firemen's Federal Credit Union is a long-established financial cooperative headquartered in Elizabeth, New Jersey, with roots tracing back to 1936—making it nearly 90 years old. The institution operates as a federal credit union with a single branch location and serves a membership base of approximately 1,133 people, primarily composed of federal, state, and local government employees with a focus on firefighting personnel.

The credit union offers core banking services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/Debit Card programs, and loan products. They provide Payday Alternative Loans (PALs)—a critical offering that provides credit union members access to small loans under 36% APR as a safer alternative to predatory payday lending. Additionally, they offer low-cost wire transfers and various secured and unsecured lending options. Their financial position shows $7.32 million in total assets, $6.22 million in member shares and deposits, and a healthy equity position of $801,596.

What distinguishes this institution is its hyper-local focus and specialized membership criteria. Rather than serving the general public, Elizabeth Firemen's targets a specific community—primarily firefighters and other government employees in the Elizabeth/Union County area. This narrow membership base creates a tight-knit financial community with governance directly accountable to members. The credit union also explicitly offers PAL loans and low-cost wire transfers, suggesting a deliberate commitment to accessible financial services for working people.

Honestly, this is a very small institution with only 3 employees and limited branch infrastructure. Members should expect minimal technological sophistication and potentially limited product variety compared to larger regional credit unions. The single-branch model and small member base mean limited convenience for those outside Elizabeth or without government employment connections. However, for qualified members seeking personalized service, fair lending practices, and community-focused banking, this institution delivers authentic relationship banking. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Loan loss allowances and credit risk management
pros: Offers Payday Alternative Loans (PALs) capped under 36% APR—a mission-driven alternative to predatory payday lenders
Provides low-cost wire transfers, reducing fees for members needing to move money
Nearly 90-year operational history (since 1936) demonstrates stability and long-term commitment to membership
Member-focused governance with elected leadership (Chairperson Thomas McNamara, CEO Fanny E. Calas) accountable to the 1,133-member base
Federal credit union status means NCUSIF deposit insurance protecting member funds
Healthy financial position with $801K in equity and positive net income of $23,641
Specialized focus on firefighters and government employees creates aligned community values and targeted service
cons: Single branch location in Elizabeth, NJ provides zero convenience for members outside the immediate area
Only 3 full and part-time employees suggest minimal staffing for member support and limited service hours
Extremely small institution ($7.3M assets) with minimal online presence (no active website listed) suggests outdated technology and limited digital banking capability
Restrictive membership requirements (federal, state, local government employees) excludes most consumers entirely
Limited loan portfolio ($2.35M total loans against $7.3M assets) suggests conservative lending that may reject qualified applicants
best_for: Firefighters and emergency responders in Elizabeth, NJ seeking relationship banking from a mission-aligned institution
Government employees (federal, state, local) in Union County wanting community-focused credit union services
Consumers needing small, emergency loans who qualify for PAL programs and want to avoid predatory payday lenders
Members seeking personalized service and direct governance accountability over convenience and digital features
diagnosis: Elizabeth Firemen's Federal Credit Union is best for firefighters and government employees in the Elizabeth, NJ area who prioritize alignment with their profession and community over modern digital banking. The critical caveat is that membership is restricted to government employees—this credit union is not available to the general public and operates as a specialized, ultra-local institution with minimal technological infrastructure.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
